{"content": "     1|# Slack Org Wiki — LLM Wiki Schema
     2|
     3|Reference inspiration:
     4|- Karpathy LLM Wiki gist: https://gist.github.com/karpathy/442a6bf555914893e9891c11519de94f
     5|
     6|## Design stance for this repo
     7|
     8|The Slack-only derived wiki remains **wiki-only**:
     9|- ✅ structured LLM-organized pages
    10|- ✅ derived facts, decisions, actions, open questions
    11|- ✅ channel/time/source metadata
    12|- ❌ raw Slack transcripts
    13|- ❌ verbatim message dumps
    14|- ❌ copied RTS payloads
    15|- ❌ any use of Slack data for model training
    16|
    17|When the unified `company-wiki` pipeline is used, raw Slack snapshots may also
    18|exist under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/slack/`, but derived wiki pages still
    19|must not embed raw source bodies directly.
    20|
    21|## Shared contract root
    22|
    23|This lane is governed by the shared docs-root contract under:
    24|- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/index.md`
    25|- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/log.md`
    26|- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/taxonomy.md`
    27|
    28|This file remains the Slack lane schema overlay for that shared contract.
    29|
    30|## Contract enforcement
    31|
    32|The mutation-capable Slack wiki build entrypoint must fail fast when the shared
    33|docs-root contract or this schema file is missing:
    34|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_build.py`
    35|
    36|## Canonical directory layout
    37|
    38|Slack-derived pages now land in the canonical company wiki root:
    39|
    40|```text
    41|workspace/company-wiki/
    42|  SCHEMA.md
    43|  index.md
    44|  log.md
    45|  raw/slack/
    46|  entities/
    47|  concepts/
    48|  comparisons/
    49|  queries/
    50|  _build/
    51|```
    52|
    53|## Structured summary input contract
    54|
    55|`workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_build.py` is now a wrapper into canonical `company-wiki/wiki_build.py`, and expects JSON shaped like either:
    56|
    57|```json
    58|{
    59|  "entries": [ ... ]
    60|}
    61|```
    62|
    63|or a top-level array of entries.
    64|
    65|Each entry should look like:
    66|
    67|```json
    68|{
    69|  "title": "Product priority drift",
    70|  "topic": "product-priority",
    71|  "summary": "Discussion suggests the roadmap is slipping toward reactive support work.",
    72|  "facts": [
    73|    "Multiple channels referenced launch uncertainty",
    74|    "Support load is displacing roadmap work"
    75|  ],
    76|  "decisions": [
    77|    "Need weekly product review"
    78|  ],
    79|  "actions": [
    80|    "Assign owner for launch readiness"
    81|  ],
    82|  "open_questions": [
    83|    "Is this concentrated in one team or org-wide?"
    84|  ],
    85|  "entities": ["product", "support", "launch"],
    86|  "channel_refs": ["C123", "C456"],
    87|  "source_refs": ["admin-inventory:2026-04-09"],
    88|  "time_range": "2026-04-01/2026-04-07",
    89|  "confidence": "medium",
    90|  "updated_at": "2026-04-09T00:00:00Z"
    91|}
    92|```
    93|
    94|## Forbidden raw-content fields
    95|
    96|The builder rejects entries containing keys such as:
    97|- `messages`
    98|- `raw_messages`
    99|- `transcript`
   100|- `raw_text`
   101|- `body`
   102|- `content`
   103|
   104|Keep inputs summarized.
   105|
   106|It also rejects undocumented top-level fields and non-string list payloads, so
   107|structured summaries cannot smuggle nested raw message data into persistent
   108|artifacts.
   109|
   110|## Generated page contract
   111|
   112|Each page contains:
   113|1. title
   114|2. updated timestamp
   115|3. summary
   116|4. key facts
   117|5. decisions
   118|6. actions
   119|7. open questions
   120|8. entities
   121|9. channel/source refs
   122|10. curation note stating that no raw Slack content is stored
   123|
   124|## Retrieval model
   125|
   126|`workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_query.py` is now a wrapper into canonical `company-wiki/wiki_query.py`:
   127|- token-match search over canonical sectioned pages
   128|- returns top matches with snippets
   129|- meant for local assistant grounding, not transcript search
   130|
   131|Future work can add embeddings if the project enables that later.
   132|
   133|## Public-channel-first flow in this workspace
   134|
   135|Current live-access flow:
   136|1. use `recent_public_channels.py` to get public channels with a recent
   137|   metadata update signal
   138|2. let the LLM organize those channels into structured summary JSON
   139|3. run `wiki_build.py`
   140|4. retrieve with `wiki_query.py`
   141|
   142|This is intentionally **LLM-organized**, not a fully automated transcript-ingest
   143|pipeline.
   144|", "total_lines": 143, "file_size": 3759, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Skill — company-wiki
     2|
     3|## Role
     4|
     5|`company-wiki` is the top-level platform-agnostic company knowledge surface.
     6|
     7|It owns:
     8|- one canonical Hermes-style root under `workspace/company-wiki/`
     9|- Slack + Trello + GitHub + Orbi source ingestion into one shared substrate
    10|- deduped raw/manifests under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/`
    11|- derived wiki generation under sectioned directories (`entities/`, `concepts/`, `comparisons/`, `queries`)
    12|- one unified local query surface
    13|- authoritative `SCHEMA.md`, `index.md`, `log.md` at the canonical root
    14|- executive-first index ordering plus preservation of manually written `queries/` and `comparisons/` pages across rebuilds
    15|
    16|Use this when the user wants:
    17|- one company wiki/search surface instead of Slack/Trello/GitHub/Orbi silos
    18|- raw snapshot caching plus AI-generated wiki refresh
    19|- a cron/manual rebuild path for the company wiki
    20|- source-specific retrieval methods documented as one operating workflow
    21|
    22|## Retrieval Methodology
    23|
    24|The company wiki is a **4-stage pipeline**:
    25|
    26|1. **Retrieve** — pull whatever each source can actually expose right now
    27|2. **Persist raw** — save retrievable source payloads under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/`
    28|3. **Normalize** — convert source-specific payloads into one shared derived-entry schema
    29|4. **Build/query** — generate/search derived wiki pages under `workspace/company-wiki/`
    30|
    31|Default retrieval/search always targets the **derived wiki**, not raw payloads.
    32|
    33|## Canonical root contract
    34|
    35|`company-wiki` is governed by the canonical root itself:
    36|- `workspace/company-wiki/SCHEMA.md`
    37|- `workspace/company-wiki/index.md`
    38|- `workspace/company-wiki/log.md`
    39|
    40|Reference-only Hermes material may remain under `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/`, but it is not authoritative for active company-wiki flow.
    41|
    42|## Source Adapters
    43|
    44|### Slack adapter
    45|
    46|Current retrieval tiers:
    47|
    48|1. **Workspace public-channel metadata**
    49|   - Uses `conversations.list`
    50|   - Captures public channel inventory, membership flags, and channel `updated` timestamps
    51|   - This is the baseline guaranteed path in this workspace
    52|
    53|2. **Public-channel message history raw**
    54|   - Uses `conversations.history` via the configured bot-token path after public-channel join
    55|   - Current refresh path attempts joins on selected public channels and persists raw history snapshots for channels where access succeeds
    56|   - Initial archive baseline runs use `--archive-mode archive` and persist history in chunked raw records plus per-channel checkpoints for resume
    57|   - Archive mode now also probes broader Slack inventory surfaces (`oversight.conversations.list`, `admin.conversations.search`) **if available** and widens candidate channels when those scopes actually work
    58|   - Hourly cron stays `incremental` only and uses per-channel latest message watermarks rather than replaying baseline pages
    59|   - This is the preferred path when the goal is a real content-backed company wiki refresh rather than metadata-only bootstrap
    60|   - Coverage is still partial and capability-dependent per channel/scope, so manifests must record successes and blockers explicitly
    61|
    62|3. **Private/admin inventory**
    63|   - Only allowed when the exact API path/scopes are verified
    64|   - Must never be implied from CLI login alone
    65|
    66|Excluded:
    67|- DM / 1:1
    68|- any unverified private-channel message visibility
    69|
    70|### GitHub adapter
    71|
    72|Current retrieval order:
    73|
    74|1. `gh auth status` — auth/scope sanity check
    75|2. `gh repo list <org>` — visible repository inventory and recent push activity
    76|3. normalized repo summaries — top active execution surfaces promoted into canonical concepts/entities pages
    77|
    78|GitHub remains strictly **read-only** and is currently sourced through the local `gh` CLI path.
    79|
    80|### Orbi adapter
    81|
    82|Current retrieval order:
    83|
    84|1. public trending endpoint — `/api/v1/board/search/realtime`
    85|2. public hot-posts list — `/list/hot?page=1`
    86|3. normalized Orbi summaries — signal overview + trending-keyword pages + hot-post pages in the canonical wiki
    87|
    88|Orbi remains strictly **public-web read-only** in this workflow. It is a live external demand/sentiment surface, not an internal operating system.
    89|
    90|### Trello adapter
    91|
    92|Current retrieval order:
    93|
    94|1. `members/me` — auth/identity sanity check
    95|2. `members/me/organizations` — visible workspace/org list
    96|3. `members/me/boards` — visible board inventory
    97|4. `boards/<id>` — board detail
    98|5. `boards/<id>/members` — board member coverage
    99|6. `boards/<id>/cards` — board card snapshot
   100|
   101|Trello remains strictly **read-only**.
   102|
   103|## Raw Snapshot Contract
   104|
   105|Raw payloads live under:
   106|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/slack/`
   107|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/trello/`
   108|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/github/`
   109|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/orbi/`
   110|
   111|Each source keeps:
   112|- `items/` — deduped raw payload records
   113|- `dedupe-index.json` — payload fingerprint index
   114|- `runs/manifest-*.json` — per-run source manifest
   115|- `channel-checkpoints/*.json` — per-channel archive/incremental resume state for Slack history capture
   116|
   117|Top-level run manifests live under:
   118|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/run-manifests/`
   119|
   120|Rules:
   121|- raw is source-labeled
   122|- raw is snapshot-based, not event-sourced
   123|- repeated identical payloads should dedupe rather than multiply
   124|- Slack history payloads are persisted as chunked `channel_history_chunk` raw items rather than one large per-channel blob
   125|- raw may be incomplete if source capability is incomplete; blockers must be recorded explicitly
   126|
   127|## Normalize Contract
   128|
   129|Normalized entries are derived from raw snapshots and must preserve:
   130|- `source_type`
   131|- `access_scope`
   132|- `source_refs`
   133|- `raw_manifest_ref`
   134|- factual summary fields (`facts`, `decisions`, `actions`, `open_questions`, etc.)
   135|
   136|What normalization does:
   137|- maps source-specific payloads into one shared wiki-entry shape
   138|- preserves provenance and caveats
   139|- avoids leaking raw bodies into derived page content
   140|
   141|## Query Semantics
   142|
   143|Default query path:
   144|
   145|```bash
   146|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_query.py \
   147|  --root workspace/company-wiki \
   148|  --query "product risk"
   149|```
   150|
   151|Query semantics:
   152|- searches Hermes-style derived sections:
   153|  - `entities/`
   154|  - `concepts/`
   155|  - `comparisons/`
   156|  - `queries/`
   157|- does not search raw payload bodies by default
   158|- returns mixed-source results in one surface
   159|- source provenance is preserved inside the page content/refs
   160|
   161|Interpretation rules:
   162|- a Slack page reflects what was retrievable from Slack at refresh time
   163|- a Trello page reflects current-state Trello snapshots at refresh time
   164|- mixed-source hits should be read as one company-memory surface, not as identical-confidence evidence
   165|
   166|## Executive realignment / audit mode
   167|
   168|Use this mode when the user asks to "reorganize the wiki", "re-rank the wiki", "pull urgent action items", or otherwise wants a management-facing readout rather than a plain query result.
   169|
   170|Working rule:
   171|- do not stop at listing current pages
   172|- inspect the canonical root as an operating product and judge whether it is decision-ready
   173|
   174|Audit sequence:
   175|1. Read `workspace/company-wiki/index.md`, `SCHEMA.md`, and the latest run manifest under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/run-manifests/`.
   176|2. Check whether the front page is action-first or merely entity-first.
   177|3. Count whether `queries/` and `comparisons/` are populated; if they are empty, call that out explicitly as a leadership-surface gap.
   178|4. Cross-check latest Slack/Trello refresh evidence against derived page timestamps to detect summary lag.
   179|5. Check for migration/hygiene debt in canonical pages:
   180|   - absolute `/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw` path leakage
   181|   - frontmatter/type mistakes
   182|   - stale timestamps after successful refresh
   183|6. Sanity-check whether the requested "all sources" surface is actually present. In current workspace reality, `company-wiki` is canonical for Slack + Trello + GitHub + Orbi; pull `github-org-intel` or deeper Orbi MCP retrieval separately only when the canonical refresh is not enough.
   184|7. Write at least one management-facing page under `workspace/company-wiki/queries/` when the result is substantive.
   185|8. Reorder `workspace/company-wiki/index.md` so decision surfaces appear before raw entity inventories.
   186|9. Preserve that manually written `queries/` page across rebuilds; do not let the next auto-build wipe the executive memo.
   187|
   188|Default executive ordering:
   189|1. `queries/` — action memos, current priorities, blocked/stale work
   190|2. `concepts/` — cross-source operating overviews
   191|3. `entities/` — boards, channels, durable subjects
   192|4. `comparisons/` — side-by-side evaluations when they exist
   193|
   194|What to emphasize in the readout:
   195|- what is moving now
   196|- what looks blocked, stale, or overloaded
   197|- what is missing from the canonical surface
   198|- the few urgent actions leadership should take next
   199|
   200|## Refresh Runbook
   201|
   202|### Prepare Slack bot membership when richer history retrieval is needed
   203|
   204|```bash
   205|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/join_public_channels.py --all-public
   206|```
   207|
   208|### Full refresh
   209|
   210|```bash
   211|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py --slack-team moveis --github-org orbisoptimus
   212|```
   213|
   214|This now attempts canonical refresh for Slack + Trello + GitHub + Orbi.
   215|
   216|### Initial public-channel archive baseline
   217|
   218|```bash
   219|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py \
   220|  --slack-team moveis \
   221|  --archive-mode archive \
   222|  --slack-channel-limit 50 \
   223|  --slack-history-limit 200 \
   224|  --slack-history-chunk-size 100
   225|```
   226|
   227|This mode:
   228|1. joins the current high-priority public channels visible to the bot token
   229|2. probes broader workspace/admin inventory if those scopes are actually available
   230|3. captures as much channel history as the current access path can actually read
   231|4. stores history as chunked raw records
   232|4. writes/updates `raw/slack/channel-checkpoints/*.json`
   233|5. resumes incomplete baseline channels on later archive runs via saved Slack cursors
   234|
   235|Honest scope note:
   236|- if admin/oversight scopes are unavailable, archive mode falls back to the public-channel, bot-accessible path and records that limitation explicitly in raw manifests
   237|
   238|### Hourly incremental refresh (cron-safe default)
   239|
   240|```bash
   241|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py \
   242|  --slack-team moveis \
   243|  --archive-mode incremental
   244|```
   245|
   246|What it does:
   247|1. captures accessible Slack source payloads
   248|2. captures accessible Trello source payloads
   249|3. writes raw source manifests
   250|4. writes a top-level run manifest

    32|def load_entries(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    33|    payload = read_json(path)
    34|    entries = payload.get('entries') if isinstance(payload, dict) else payload
    35|    if not isinstance(entries, list):
    36|        raise CompanyWikiError('Expected a top-level JSON array or an object with an entries array.')
    37|    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    38|    for item in entries:
    39|        if not isinstance(item, dict):
    40|            raise CompanyWikiError('Every entry must be a JSON object.')
    41|        ensure_entry(item)
    42|        out.append(item)
    43|    return out
    44|
    45|
    46|def classify_entry(entry: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    47|    topic = str(entry.get('topic') or '').lower()
    48|    title = str(entry.get('title') or '').lower()
    49|    source_type = str(entry.get('source_type') or '').lower()
    50|    text = ' '.join([topic, title])
    51|    if 'comparison' in text or 'vs' in text:
    52|        return 'comparisons'
    53|    if source_type == 'trello' and topic.startswith('trello-board-'):
    54|        return 'entities'
    55|    if source_type == 'slack' and topic.startswith('public-channel-'):
    56|        return 'entities'
    57|    if source_type == 'github' and topic.startswith('github-repo-'):
    58|        return 'entities'
    59|    if source_type == 'github' and topic.startswith('github-org-overview-'):
    60|        return 'concepts'
    61|    if source_type == 'orbi' and (topic.startswith('orbi-trending-keyword-') or topic.startswith('orbi-hot-post-')):
    62|        return 'entities'
    63|    if source_type == 'orbi' and topic.startswith('orbi-signal-overview'):
    64|        return 'concepts'
    65|    if 'overview' in text or topic.endswith('-overview') or 'history capture' in text:
    66|        return 'concepts'
    67|    return 'queries'
    68|
    69|
    70|def merge_entries(entries: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
    71|    grouped: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
    72|    buckets: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = defaultdict(list)
    73|    for entry in entries:
    74|        key = slugify(str(entry.get('topic') or entry.get('title')))
    75|        buckets[key].append(entry)
    76|
    77|    for key, group in buckets.items():
    78|        title = str(group[0].get('title') or group[0].get('topic') or key)
    79|        bucket = group[0]
    80|        section = classify_entry(bucket)
    81|        grouped[key] = {
    82|            'slug': key,
    83|            'title': title,
    84|            'section': section,
    85|            'summary': ' '.join(compact_unique(str(item.get('summary', '')) for item in group)),
    86|            'facts': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('facts', []) or []),
    87|            'decisions': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('decisions', []) or []),
    88|            'actions': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('actions', []) or []),
    89|            'open_questions': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('open_questions', []) or []),
    90|            'entities': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('entities', []) or []),
    91|            'channel_refs': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('channel_refs', []) or []),
    92|            'source_refs': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('source_refs', []) or []),
    93|            'source_types': compact_unique(str(item.get('source_type', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('source_type', '')).strip()),
    94|            'access_scopes': compact_unique(str(item.get('access_scope', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('access_scope', '')).strip()),
    95|            'raw_manifest_refs': compact_unique(str(item.get('raw_manifest_ref', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('raw_manifest_ref', '')).strip()),
    96|            'time_ranges': compact_unique(str(item.get('time_range', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('time_range', '')).strip()),
    97|            'confidences': compact_unique(str(item.get('confidence', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('confidence', '')).strip()),
    98|            'updated_at': compact_unique(str(item.get('updated_at', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('updated_at', '')).strip())[-1:] or [now_iso()],
    99|            'classification_rationale': f"classified as {section} from topic/title/source_type heuristics",
   100|        }
   101|    return grouped
   102|
   103|
   104|def singular_section(section: str) -> str:
   105|    mapping = {
   106|        'entities': 'entity',
   107|        'concepts': 'concept',
   108|        'comparisons': 'comparison',
   109|        'queries': 'query',
   110|    }
   111|    return mapping.get(section, section[:-1] if section.endswith('s') else section)

   113|
   114|def render_page(page: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   115|    raw_manifest_refs = [relativize_path_string(ref) for ref in page['raw_manifest_refs']]
   116|    source_refs = [relativize_path_string(ref) for ref in page['source_refs']]
   117|    frontmatter = [
   118|        '---',
   119|        f"title: {page['title']}",
   120|        f"updated: {page['updated_at'][0]}",
   121|        f"type: {singular_section(page['section'])}",
   122|        f"sources: [{', '.join(raw_manifest_refs)}]",
   123|        '---',
   124|        '',
   125|    ]
   126|    lines = [
   127|        f"# {page['title']}",
   128|        '',
   129|        f"- Updated at: {page['updated_at'][0]}",
   130|        f"- Classification: {page['section']}",
   131|        f"- Confidence: {', '.join(page['confidences']) or 'unspecified'}",
   132|        f"- Source types: {', '.join(page['source_types']) or 'unspecified'}",
   133|        f"- Access scopes: {', '.join(page['access_scopes']) or 'unspecified'}",
   134|        f"- Provenance: {', '.join(raw_manifest_refs) or 'none recorded'}",
   135|        '',
   136|        '## Summary',
   137|        page['summary'],
   138|        '',
   139|        '## Classification note',
   140|        f"- {page['classification_rationale']}",
   141|    ]
   142|    sections = [
   143|        ('Key facts', page['facts']),
   144|        ('Decisions', page['decisions']),
   145|        ('Actions', page['actions']),
   146|        ('Open questions', page['open_questions']),
   147|        ('Entities', page['entities']),
   148|        ('Channel references', page['channel_refs']),
   149|        ('Source references', source_refs),
   150|        ('Raw manifest references', raw_manifest_refs),
   151|    ]
   152|    for title, items in sections:
   153|        lines += ['', f'## {title}']
   154|        lines.extend(f'- {item}' for item in items) if items else lines.append('- none recorded')
   155|    lines += [
   156|        '',
   157|        '## Curation note',
   158|        '- This page was built from structured summaries only.',
   159|        '- Historical imported raw artifacts remain immutable even when they still contain old-root path strings.',
   160|    ]
   161|    return '\n'.join(frontmatter + lines) + '\n'

   163|
   164|def render_schema(root: Path) -> str:
   165|    canonical_root = relativize_path_string(root)
   166|    return f"""# Company Wiki Schema
   167|
   168|## Canonical root
   169|- `{canonical_root}`
   170|
   171|## Authority
   172|- This root is the only supported company wiki surface.
   173|- `SCHEMA.md`, `index.md`, and `log.md` here are authoritative.
   174|- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/*` is reference-only and unsupported for active company-wiki flow.
   175|
   176|## Reading order
   177|1. `queries/` — latest executive memo, realignment note, or retained synthesis first
   178|2. `concepts/` — source-overview pages that explain what Slack/Trello signals currently mean
   179|3. `entities/` — channel, board, repo, or other subject-level drill-down only after the executive/context pages
   180|4. `comparisons/` — explicit tradeoff pages when multiple options or surfaces are being evaluated side-by-side
   181|
   182|## Coverage model
   183|- Auto-built canonical pages currently come from normalized Slack + Trello + GitHub snapshots.
   184|- Orbi MCP, repo skills/docs, and cron/runtime state remain valid orientation sources for manual wiki audits even when they are not yet auto-ingested into normalized entries.
   185|- Derived pages must keep provenance to manifests, source refs, or clearly named live evidence.
   186|- Raw source bodies stay under `raw/`; derived pages should stay summary-first.
   187|
   188|## Layout
   189|- `raw/` — immutable source snapshots, imported history, and manifests
   190|- `entities/` — durable subjects
   191|- `concepts/` — durable operating knowledge
   192|- `comparisons/` — side-by-side evaluations
   193|- `queries/` — retained syntheses and fallback classification bucket
   194|- `_build/` — generated manifests, normalized inputs, cutover metadata
   195|
   196|## Classification fallback
   197|- If an entry does not fit `entities/`, `concepts/`, or `comparisons/`, it lands in `queries/`.
   198|"""
   199|
   200|
   201|def sort_pages_for_section(section: str, items: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   202|    if section == 'queries':
   203|        return sorted(items, key=lambda item: (str(item['updated_at']), str(item['title']).lower()), reverse=True)
   204|    return sorted(items, key=lambda item: str(item['title']).lower())
   205|
   206|
   207|def render_index(pages_by_section: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]]) -> str:
   208|    total = sum(len(items) for items in pages_by_section.values())
   209|    section_counts = {section: len(pages_by_section.get(section, [])) for section in SECTION_DIRS}
   210|    latest_queries = sort_pages_for_section('queries', pages_by_section.get('queries', []))[:3]
   211|    concept_overviews = sort_pages_for_section('concepts', pages_by_section.get('concepts', []))[:3]
   212|    lines = [
   213|        '# Company Wiki Index',
   214|        '',
   215|        '> Executive-first catalog for the canonical company wiki.',
   216|        '> Read queries first, concepts second, entities last.',
   217|        '',
   218|        f'- Built at: {now_iso()}',
   219|        f'- Page count: {total}',
   220|        f"- Section counts: queries={section_counts['queries']}, concepts={section_counts['concepts']}, entities={section_counts['entities']}, comparisons={section_counts['comparisons']}",
   221|        '',
   222|        '## Start here',
   223|    ]
   224|    if latest_queries:
   225|        for page in latest_queries:
   226|            lines.append(f"- Executive memo: [{page['title']}](queries/{page['slug']}.md) — updated {page['updated_at']}")
   227|    else:
   228|        lines.append('- Executive memo: none yet')
   229|    if concept_overviews:
   230|        for page in concept_overviews:
   231|            lines.append(f"- Source overview: [{page['title']}](concepts/{page['slug']}.md) — updated {page['updated_at']}")
   232|    else:
   233|        lines.append('- Source overview: none yet')
   234|    lines += [
   235|        '- Drill into `entities/` only after the executive memo and source-overview pages.',
   236|        '',
   237|        '## Source surface map',
   238|        '- Slack operating signal — auto-ingested today into canonical pages via raw manifests and derived summaries.',
   239|        '- Trello planning signal — auto-ingested today into canonical pages via read-only REST snapshots.',
   240|        '- GitHub execution signal — auto-ingested today into canonical pages via read-only gh CLI snapshots.',
   241|        '- Orbi signal — auto-ingested today into canonical pages via public trending and hot-post snapshots.',
   242|        '- Skills/docs/cron — routing, governance, and automation context for how the wiki should be read and refreshed.',
   243|    ]
   244|    for section in ('queries', 'concepts', 'entities', 'comparisons'):
   245|        lines += ['', f'## {section.title()}']
   246|        items = sort_pages_for_section(section, pages_by_section.get(section, []))
   247|        if not items:
   248|            lines.append('- none')
   249|            continue
   250|        for page in items:
   251|            lines.append(f"- [{page['title']}]({section}/{page['slug']}.md) — updated {page['updated_at']}")
   252|    return '\n'.join(lines) + '\n'

   255|def clean_section_dirs(root_paths: dict[str, Path]) -> None:
   256|    for section in SECTION_DIRS:
   257|        section_dir = root_paths[section]
   258|        if section_dir.exists():
   259|            shutil.rmtree(section_dir)
   260|        section_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

   262|
   263|def frontmatter_field(text: str, field: str) -> str:
   264|    match = re.search(rf'^{re.escape(field)}:\s*(.+)$', text, re.MULTILINE)
   265|    return match.group(1).strip() if match else ''
   266|
   267|
   268|def load_preserved_pages(root_paths: dict[str, Path], generated_slugs: set[str]) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
   269|    preserved: list[dict[str, str]] = []
   270|    for section in SECTION_DIRS:
   271|        section_dir = root_paths[section]
   272|        if not section_dir.exists():
   273|            continue
   274|        for path in sorted(section_dir.glob('*.md')):
   275|            slug = path.stem
   276|            if slug in generated_slugs:
   277|                continue
   278|            text = path.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   279|            title = frontmatter_field(text, 'title') or slug.replace('-', ' ')
   280|            updated_at = frontmatter_field(text, 'updated') or now_iso()
   281|            preserved.append({
   282|                'slug': slug,
   283|                'title': title,
   284|                'section': section,
   285|                'updated_at': updated_at,
   286|                'text': text,
   287|            })
   288|    return preserved

   291|def main() -> None:
   292|    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Build the canonical Hermes-style company wiki from structured summary JSON.')
   293|    parser.add_argument('--input', required=True, help='Path to structured summary JSON.')
   294|    parser.add_argument('--root', default=str(COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT), help='Canonical company wiki root.')
   295|    args = parser.parse_args()
   296|
   297|    root = Path(args.root)
   298|    bootstrap_company_wiki_root(root)
   299|    ensure_contract_ready('company-wiki', root=root)
   300|    root_paths = company_wiki_paths(root)
   301|    entries = load_entries(Path(args.input))
   302|    merged = merge_entries(entries)
   303|    preserved_pages = load_preserved_pages(root_paths, set(merged.keys()))
   304|    clean_section_dirs(root_paths)
   305|
   306|    manifest_pages: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   307|    pages_by_section: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = defaultdict(list)
   308|    for slug, page in merged.items():
   309|        section_dir = root_paths[page['section']]
   310|        page_path = section_dir / f'{slug}.md'
   311|        write_text(page_path, render_page(page))
   312|        record = {
   313|            'slug': slug,
   314|            'title': page['title'],
   315|            'path': str(page_path),
   316|            'updated_at': page['updated_at'][0],
   317|            'source_types': page['source_types'],
   318|            'section': page['section'],
   319|        }
   320|        manifest_pages.append(record)
   321|        pages_by_section[page['section']].append(record)
   322|
   323|    for page in preserved_pages:
   324|        section_dir = root_paths[page['section']]
   325|        page_path = section_dir / f"{page['slug']}.md"
   326|        write_text(page_path, page['text'])
   327|        record = {
   328|            'slug': page['slug'],
   329|            'title': page['title'],
   330|            'path': str(page_path),
   331|            'updated_at': page['updated_at'],
   332|            'source_types': ['manual'],
   333|            'section': page['section'],

    90|def now_iso() -> str:

    92|

    94|def slugify(value: str) -> str:
    95|    value = value.strip().lower()
    96|    value = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '-', value)
    97|    return value.strip('-') or 'untitled'

   100|def compact_unique(items: Iterable[str]) -> list[str]:
   101|    seen: set[str] = set()
   102|    out: list[str] = []
   103|    for item in items:
   104|        value = str(item).strip()
   105|        if not value or value in seen:
   106|            continue
   107|        seen.add(value)
   108|        out.append(value)
   109|    return out

   112|def normalize_key(value: str) -> str:
   113|    return re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '', str(value).lower())

   116|def read_json(path: Path) -> Any:
   117|    return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
   118|
   119|
   120|def write_json(path: Path, payload: Any) -> None:
   121|    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   122|    path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2) + '\n', encoding='utf-8')
   123|
   124|
   125|def write_text(path: Path, text: str) -> None:
   126|    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   127|    path.write_text(text, encoding='utf-8')
   128|
   129|
   130|def relativize_path_string(value: Any, *, root: Path | None = None) -> str:
   131|    text = str(value or '').strip()
   132|    if not text:
   133|        return text
   134|    if '://' in text:
   135|        return text
   136|    path = Path(text).expanduser()
   137|    candidates: list[Path] = []
   138|    if root is not None:
   139|        candidates.append(root.expanduser())
   140|    candidates.append(REPO_ROOT)
   141|    for candidate in candidates:
   142|        try:
   143|            return path.relative_to(candidate).as_posix()
   144|        except Exception:
   145|            pass
   146|        try:
   147|            return path.resolve().relative_to(candidate.resolve()).as_posix()
   148|        except Exception:
   149|            pass
   150|    return text
   151|
   152|
   153|def company_wiki_paths(root: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, Path]:
   154|    canonical_root = (root or COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT).expanduser()
   155|    return {
   156|        'root': canonical_root,
   157|        'schema': canonical_root / 'SCHEMA.md',
   158|        'index': canonical_root / 'index.md',
   159|        'log': canonical_root / 'log.md',
   160|        'raw': canonical_root / 'raw',
   161|        'entities': canonical_root / 'entities',
   162|        'concepts': canonical_root / 'concepts',
   163|        'comparisons': canonical_root / 'comparisons',
   164|        'queries': canonical_root / 'queries',
   165|        'build': canonical_root / '_build',
   166|        'build_normalized': canonical_root / '_build' / 'normalized',
   167|        'build_manifest': canonical_root / '_build' / 'manifest.json',
   168|        'cutover_map': canonical_root / 'raw' / 'cutover-map.json',
   169|    }
   170|
   171|
   172|def default_company_schema() -> str:
   173|    return """# Company Wiki Schema
   174|
   175|## Authority
   176|- Canonical root: `workspace/company-wiki/`
   177|- This root is the only supported company wiki surface.
   178|- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/*` is reference-only and unsupported for active company-wiki flow.
   179|
   180|## Layout
   181|- `raw/` — immutable source snapshots and manifests
   182|- `entities/` — durable subjects (channels, boards, teams, systems)
   183|- `concepts/` — durable themes, workflows, operating patterns
   184|- `comparisons/` — side-by-side evaluations
   185|- `queries/` — retained synthesis pages and fallback classification bucket
   186|- `_build/` — generated manifests, normalized intermediates, and cutover metadata
   187|
   188|## Rules
   189|- Do not mutate imported historical raw artifacts in place.
   190|- Preserve provenance from derived pages back into canonical `raw/`.
   191|- When classification is ambiguous, default to `queries/`.
   192|"""
   193|
   194|
   195|def bootstrap_company_wiki_root(root: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, str]:
   196|    paths = company_wiki_paths(root)
   197|    for key in ('root', 'raw', 'entities', 'concepts', 'comparisons', 'queries', 'build', 'build_normalized'):
   198|        paths[key].mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   199|    if not paths['schema'].exists():
   200|        write_text(paths['schema'], default_company_schema())
   201|    if not paths['index'].exists():
   202|        write_text(paths['index'], '# Company Wiki Index\n\n- Built at: never\n- Page count: 0\n')
   203|    if not paths['log'].exists():
   204|        write_text(paths['log'], '# Company Wiki Log\n\n')
   205|    return {key: str(value) for key, value in paths.items()}
   206|

   208|def contract_docs_root() -> Path:
   209|    override = os.environ.get(DOCS_ROOT_ENV, '').strip()
   210|    return Path(override).expanduser() if override else REPO_ROOT / 'workspace' / 'docs'
   211|

   214|    if lane == 'company-wiki':

   238|
   239|def ensure_contract_ready(lane: str, root: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, str]:

   242|    requirements = contract_requirements(lane, root)

{"content": "     1|#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2|from __future__ import annotations
     3|
     4|import json
     5|import subprocess
     6|from collections import Counter
     7|from datetime import datetime, timezone
     8|from pathlib import Path
     9|from typing import Any
    10|
    11|from _common import CompanyWikiError, now_iso
    12|from raw_store import persist_raw_payload, write_run_manifest
    13|
    14|UTC = timezone.utc
    15|
    16|
    17|def run_gh_json(args: list[str]) -> Any:
    18|    proc = subprocess.run(['gh', *args], capture_output=True, text=True)
    19|    if proc.returncode != 0:
    20|        raise CompanyWikiError((proc.stderr or proc.stdout).strip() or f"gh command failed: {' '.join(args)}")
    21|    try:
    22|        return json.loads(proc.stdout)
    23|    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
    24|        raise CompanyWikiError(f'GitHub CLI returned invalid JSON: {exc}') from exc
    25|
    26|
    27|def gh_auth_status() -> dict[str, Any]:
    28|    proc = subprocess.run(['gh', 'auth', 'status'], capture_output=True, text=True)
    29|    if proc.returncode != 0:
    30|        raise CompanyWikiError((proc.stderr or proc.stdout).strip() or 'gh auth status failed')
    31|    combined = '\n'.join(part for part in [proc.stderr.strip(), proc.stdout.strip()] if part).strip()
    32|    scopes: list[str] = []
    33|    for line in combined.splitlines():
    34|        if 'Token scopes:' in line:
    35|            scopes_text = line.split('Token scopes:', 1)[1].strip().strip("'")
    36|            scopes = [part.strip().strip("'") for part in scopes_text.split(',') if part.strip()]
    37|            break
    38|    return {'status': 'ok', 'scopes': scopes, 'raw': combined}
    39|
    40|
    41|def fetch_repos(org: str, *, limit: int = 200) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    42|    repos = run_gh_json([
    43|        'repo', 'list', org,
    44|        '--limit', str(limit),
    45|        '--json', 'name,nameWithOwner,description,isPrivate,isArchived,isFork,pushedAt,updatedAt,createdAt,primaryLanguage,stargazerCount,forkCount,url,defaultBranchRef',
    46|    ])
    47|    if not isinstance(repos, list):
    48|        raise CompanyWikiError('GitHub repo list must be a JSON array.')
    49|    return repos
    50|
    51|
    52|def stream_for_repo(name: str) -> str:
    53|    lowered = name.lower()
    54|    if lowered in {'atom.io', 'orbi-advance'} or 'backend' in lowered or 'platform' in lowered:
    55|        return 'core-platform'
    56|    if lowered in {'orbi_app', 'orbi_class_app', 'orbi_common_flutter'} or 'flutter' in lowered or 'ios' in lowered or 'android' in lowered:
    57|        return 'mobile'
    58|    if lowered in {'orbi-oracle-bot', 'orbi-viewer-mcp', 'move-ai', 'ai-math'} or 'bot' in lowered or 'mcp' in lowered:
    59|        return 'ai-assistant'
    60|    if lowered in {'tutor', 'spica'} or 'tutor' in lowered or 'class' in lowered:
    61|        return 'product-education'
    62|    if 'archive' in lowered or 'legacy' in lowered or 'deprecated' in lowered:
    63|        return 'legacy-cleanup'
    64|    return 'unclassified'
    65|
    66|
    67|def active_counts(repos: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, int]:
    68|    now = datetime.now(UTC)
    69|    counts = {'30d': 0, '90d': 0, '365d': 0}
    70|    for repo in repos:
    71|        pushed = str(repo.get('pushedAt') or '').strip()
    72|        if not pushed:
    73|            continue
    74|        dt = datetime.fromisoformat(pushed.replace('Z', '+00:00'))
    75|        age_days = (now - dt).days
    76|        if age_days <= 30:
    77|            counts['30d'] += 1
    78|        if age_days <= 90:
    79|            counts['90d'] += 1
    80|        if age_days <= 365:
    81|            counts['365d'] += 1
    82|    return counts
    83|
    84|
    85|def repos_active_within_days(repos: list[dict[str, Any]], *, days: int) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    86|    now = datetime.now(UTC)
    87|    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    88|    for repo in repos:
    89|        if repo.get('isArchived'):
    90|            continue
    91|        pushed = str(repo.get('pushedAt') or '').strip()
    92|        if not pushed:
    93|            continue
    94|        dt = datetime.fromisoformat(pushed.replace('Z', '+00:00'))
    95|        if (now - dt).days <= days:
    96|            out.append(repo)
    97|    return sorted(out, key=lambda repo: str(repo.get('pushedAt') or ''), reverse=True)
    98|
    99|
   100|def build_entries(raw_manifest_ref: str, org: str, auth: dict[str, Any], repos: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   101|    active = active_counts(repos)
   102|    non_archived = [repo for repo in repos if not repo.get('isArchived')]
   103|    active_30d = repos_active_within_days(repos, days=30)
   104|    recently_active = sorted(non_archived, key=lambda repo: str(repo.get('pushedAt') or ''), reverse=True)
   105|    stream_counts = Counter(stream_for_repo(str(repo.get('name') or '')) for repo in non_archived)
   106|    branch_counts = Counter((((repo.get('defaultBranchRef') or {}) or {}).get('name') or 'none') for repo in repos)
   107|    index_repos = active_30d or recently_active[:8]
   108|
   109|    overview = {
   110|        'title': f'GitHub portfolio overview — {org}',
   111|        'topic': f'github-org-overview-{org}',
   112|        'summary': f'GitHub CLI read-only snapshot shows {len(repos)} visible repositories for {org}, with current execution concentrated in the recently active repositories.',
   113|        'facts': [
   114|            f'Visible repos: {len(repos)}',
   115|            f'Private repos: {sum(1 for repo in repos if repo.get("isPrivate"))}',
   116|            f'Archived repos: {sum(1 for repo in repos if repo.get("isArchived"))}',
   117|            f'Active in last 30 days: {active["30d"]}',
   118|            f'Active in last 90 days: {active["90d"]}',
   119|            f'Dominant default branches: {", ".join(f"{branch}:{count}" for branch, count in branch_counts.most_common(5))}',
   120|            f'Stream mapping: {", ".join(f"{stream}:{count}" for stream, count in sorted(stream_counts.items()))}',
   121|        ],
   122|        'decisions': ['Treat GitHub as a first-class source in the canonical company wiki, not a separate side report.'],
   123|        'actions': [
   124|            'Keep every repo active in the last 30 days visible in the company-wiki entity index.',
   125|            'Deep-dive the top active repos before making staffing or roadmap calls.',
   126|        ],
   127|        'open_questions': ['Which active repos should promote into named company streams instead of staying heuristic?'],
   128|        'entities': [org] + [str(repo.get('name')) for repo in index_repos if repo.get('name')],
   129|        'channel_refs': [str(repo.get('nameWithOwner')) for repo in index_repos if repo.get('nameWithOwner')],
   130|        'source_refs': [f'github:org:{org}'],
   131|        'time_range': now_iso().split('T')[0],
   132|        'confidence': 'high' if {'repo', 'read:org'} & set(auth.get('scopes') or []) else 'medium',
   133|        'updated_at': now_iso(),
   134|        'source_type': 'github',
   135|        'access_scope': 'gh_cli_read_only',
   136|        'raw_manifest_ref': raw_manifest_ref,
   137|    }
   138|    entries = [overview]
   139|
   140|    for repo in index_repos:
   141|        name = str(repo.get('name') or '').strip()
   142|        full_name = str(repo.get('nameWithOwner') or name)
   143|        if not name:
   144|            continue
   145|        language = ((repo.get('primaryLanguage') or {}) or {}).get('name') or 'Unknown'
   146|        pushed_at = str(repo.get('pushedAt') or 'unknown')
   147|        default_branch = (((repo.get('defaultBranchRef') or {}) or {}).get('name') or 'none')
   148|        entries.append({
   149|            'title': f'GitHub repo — {full_name}',
   150|            'topic': f'github-repo-{name}',
   151|            'summary': f'{full_name} is currently in the recent-active set for the org, with latest push at {pushed_at} and primary language {language}.',
   152|            'facts': [
   153|                f'Repo: {full_name}',
   154|                f'Pushed at: {pushed_at}',
   155|                f'Updated at: {repo.get("updatedAt") or "unknown"}',
   156|                f'Default branch: {default_branch}',
   157|                f'Primary language: {language}',
   158|                f'Archived: {bool(repo.get("isArchived"))}',
   159|                f'Private: {bool(repo.get("isPrivate"))}',
   160|            ],
   161|            'decisions': ['Treat GitHub repo pages as read-only execution snapshots.'],
   162|            'actions': ['Use repo-level deep dives when this repository becomes a current priority.'],
   163|            'open_questions': ['Does this repo need owner/stream labeling in the company wiki?'],
   164|            'entities': [name, stream_for_repo(name)],
   165|            'channel_refs': [full_name],
   166|            'source_refs': [f'github:repo:{full_name}', str(repo.get('url') or '')],
   167|            'time_range': now_iso().split('T')[0],
   168|            'confidence': 'high',
   169|            'updated_at': now_iso(),
   170|            'source_type': 'github',
   171|            'access_scope': 'gh_cli_read_only',
   172|            'raw_manifest_ref': raw_manifest_ref,
   173|        })
   174|    return entries
   175|
   176|
   177|def capture(*, raw_root: Path, org: str = 'orbisoptimus', limit: int = 200) -> dict[str, Any]:
   178|    github_root = raw_root / 'github'
   179|    collections: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   180|    auth = gh_auth_status()
   181|    repos = fetch_repos(org, limit=limit)
   182|    auth_item = persist_raw_payload(source_root=github_root, payload_kind='gh_auth_status', source_id=f'gh-auth:{org}', payload=auth, access_scope='gh_cli_read_only')
   183|    repos_item = persist_raw_payload(source_root=github_root, payload_kind='repo_list', source_id=f'gh-repos:{org}', payload={'org': org, 'repos': repos}, access_scope='gh_cli_read_only')
   184|    collections.append({'kind': 'gh_auth_status', 'items': [auth_item]})
   185|    collections.append({'kind': 'repo_list', 'items': [repos_item]})
   186|    manifest_path = write_run_manifest(
   187|        source_root=github_root,
   188|        generated_at=now_iso(),
   189|        status='ok',
   190|        access_scope='gh_cli_read_only',
   191|        collections=collections,
   192|        extra={'org': org, 'repo_count': len(repos)},
   193|    )
   194|    return {
   195|        'status': 'ok',
   196|        'manifest': str(manifest_path),
   197|        'entries': build_entries(str(manifest_path), org, auth, repos),
   198|    }

{"content": "     1|from __future__ import annotations
     2|
     3|from pathlib import Path
     4|from typing import Any
     5|
     6|from _common import hash_payload, now_iso, read_json, write_json
     7|
     8|
     9|def persist_raw_payload(
    10|    *,
    11|    source_root: Path,
    12|    payload_kind: str,
    13|    source_id: str,
    14|    payload: Any,
    15|    access_scope: str,
    16|) -> dict[str, Any]:
    17|    items_dir = source_root / 'items'
    18|    items_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    19|    index_path = source_root / 'dedupe-index.json'
    20|    index: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = read_json(index_path) if index_path.exists() else {}
    21|
    22|    dedupe_key = hash_payload({'payload_kind': payload_kind, 'source_id': source_id, 'payload': payload})
    23|    existing = index.get(dedupe_key)
    24|    created = False
    25|    if existing is None:
    26|        item_path = items_dir / f'{dedupe_key}.json'
    27|        record = {
    28|            'dedupe_key': dedupe_key,
    29|            'payload_kind': payload_kind,
    30|            'source_id': source_id,
    31|            'access_scope': access_scope,
    32|            'first_seen_at': now_iso(),
    33|            'last_seen_at': now_iso(),
    34|            'payload': payload,
    35|        }
    36|        write_json(item_path, record)
    37|        existing = {
    38|            'path': str(item_path),
    39|            'payload_kind': payload_kind,
    40|            'source_id': source_id,
    41|            'first_seen_at': record['first_seen_at'],
    42|            'last_seen_at': record['last_seen_at'],
    43|            'access_scope': access_scope,
    44|        }
    45|        index[dedupe_key] = existing
    46|        created = True
    47|    else:
    48|        item_path = Path(existing['path'])
    49|        record = read_json(item_path)
    50|        record['last_seen_at'] = now_iso()
    51|        write_json(item_path, record)
    52|        existing['last_seen_at'] = record['last_seen_at']
    53|        index[dedupe_key] = existing
    54|
    55|    write_json(index_path, index)
    56|    return {
    57|        'dedupe_key': dedupe_key,

    66|def write_run_manifest(
    67|    *,
    68|    source_root: Path,
    69|    generated_at: str,
    70|    status: str,
    71|    access_scope: str,
    72|    collections: list[dict[str, Any]],
    73|    blocker: str | None = None,
    74|    extra: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
    75|) -> Path:
    76|    runs_dir = source_root / 'runs'
    77|    runs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    78|    path = runs_dir / f'manifest-{generated_at.replace(":", "").replace("-", "")}.json'
    79|    payload = {
    80|        'generated_at': generated_at,
    81|        'status': status,
    82|        'access_scope': access_scope,
    83|        'collection_count': len(collections),
    84|        'created_count': sum(1 for collection in collections for item in collection.get('items', []) if item.get('created')),
    85|        'deduped_count': sum(1 for collection in collections for item in collection.get('items', []) if not item.get('created')),
    86|        'collections': collections,
    87|    }
    88|    if blocker:
    89|        payload['blocker'] = blocker
    90|    if extra:
    91|        payload.update(extra)
    92|    write_json(path, payload)
    93|    return path

import json, os, urllib.request
key=os.environ['NOTION_API_KEY']

})

        title=''.join(rt.get('plain_text','') for rt in item.get('title',[]))
        print(obj, iid, title[:80], url)

print('next_cursor=', bool(data.get('next_cursor')))

{"command":"set -euo pipefail
set -a
source "$HOME/.hermes/.env"
set +a
python3 - <<'PY'
import json, os, urllib.request
key=os.environ['NOTION_API_KEY']
headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {key}','Notion-Version':'2025-09-03','Content-Type':'application/json'}
url='https://api.notion.com/v1/search'
cursor=None
count=0
pages=0
datasources=0
while True:
    payload={'page_size':100}
    if cursor: payload['start_cursor']=cursor
    req=urllib.request.Request(url, data=json.dumps(payload).encode(), headers=headers)
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as r:
        data=json.load(r)
    results=data.get('results',[])
    count += len(results)
    for item in results:
        if item.get('object')=='page': pages += 1
        elif item.get('object')=='data_source': datasources += 1
    if not data.get('has_more'):
        break
    cursor=data.get('next_cursor')
print(json.dumps({'total':count,'pages':pages,'data_sources':datasources}, ensure_ascii=False))
PY","timeout":120}

    23|  - live: Orbi trending searches snapshot (2026-04-13)
    24|---
    25|
    26|# 2026-04-13 initial wiki reordering
    27|
    28|## Situation
    29|초기 위키는 Slack/Trello 수집은 붙어 있는데, 첫 화면이 아직 **엔티티 나열형**이다. 운영 리드가 바로 읽어야 할 순서는 엔티티가 아니라 **판단 메모 → 소스 개요 → 개별 대상**이다.
    30|
    31|## Evidence snapshot
    32|- Slack 최신 run manifest 기준 public channels 7개 join 성공, history capture 성공 7개, chunk 저장은 `#서비스-배포-알림`, `#ai_news`에서 확인된다.
    33|- Trello 최신 manifest 기준 visible workspace 1개, open boards 4개, collection_count 6이다.
    34|- GitHub live surface는 정상 인증돼 있고 최근 업데이트 상위 repo는 `orbi-oracle-bot`, `ai-math`, `tutor`, `orbi-viewer-mcp`, `spica` 순으로 보인다.
    35|- Orbi live surface는 hot posts와 trending searches가 현재 응답한다. 반면 MCP generic list endpoints는 이 브리지에서 직접 노출되지 않았다.
    36|- 시스템 crontab에는 `17 * * * * ... run_company_wiki_refresh.sh`가 살아 있다.
    37|- skill/router 기준 회사 위키 해석면은 `company-wiki`, Slack 수집 lane은 `slack-org-wiki`, GitHub 실행면은 `github-org-intel`, Trello exec 해석면은 `trello-wiki-exec-report`, 자동화 문맥은 `create_slack_cron`이 담당한다.
    38|
    39|## Analysis
    40|### 1. 첫 화면은 executive-first가 맞다
    41|대표/운영 리드가 처음 봐야 하는 건 “무슨 소스가 살아 있고”, “무엇이 자동 반영되고”, “어디까지가 아직 수동 해석 구간인지”다.
    42|
    43|### 2. canonical auto-ingest와 live-access surface를 분리해서 보여줘야 한다
    44|현재 canonical auto-ingest는 Slack+Trello다. 하지만 실제 운영상 사용 가능한 리소스는 GitHub, Orbi, skill/docs, cron까지 넓다. 이 차이를 인덱스와 스키마에서 명시해야 오해가 줄어든다.
    45|
    46|### 3. 위키 hygiene는 바로잡아야 했다
    47|빌더가 `entities -> entitie`로 잘못 단수화하고 있었고, 인덱스도 단순 section dump였다. 이건 검색 품질과 신뢰를 같이 깎는다.
    48|
    49|## Reordered reading path
    50|1. `queries/` — 최신 판단 메모
    51|2. `concepts/` — Slack/Trello source overview
    52|3. `entities/` — channel/board drill-down
    53|4. `comparisons/` — 선택지 비교가 생길 때 추가
    54|
    55|## Recommendation
    56|- company wiki의 기본 독서 순서를 executive-first로 고정한다.
    57|- 인덱스에 Slack/Trello auto-ingest, GitHub/Orbi live surface, skill/docs/cron context를 분리 표기한다.
    58|- 수동 판단 메모는 보존 대상으로 취급해서 다음 빌드에도 살아남게 해야 한다.
    59|
    60|## Result applied in this cycle
    61|- `wiki_build.py`에서 `type: entity` 단수화 버그를 수정했다.
    62|- `SCHEMA.md`에 reading order와 coverage model을 추가했다.
    63|- `index.md`를 executive-first landing 구조로 재작성했다.
    64|- 수동 query/comparison 페이지가 빌드 시 사라지지 않도록 preservation 로직을 추가했다.
    65|", "total_lines": 64, "file_size": 3867, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

I’ll implement functions like `request_json`, `search_all`, and `collect_block_children`, which might require recursive traversal. Also, I'll consider how to paginate results from `query_data_source`. It seems like a lot, but I believe it’ll work out!

{"path":"workspace/bin/company-wiki/notion_snapshot.py","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
import time
import urllib.error
import urllib.request
from collections import Counter
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

from _common import CompanyWikiError, now_iso, slugify
from raw_store import persist_raw_payload, write_run_manifest

NOTION_VERSION = '2025-09-03'
API_BASE = 'https://api.notion.com/v1'
REQUEST_DELAY_SECONDS = 0.35
SEARCH_PAGE_SIZE = 100
BLOCK_PAGE_SIZE = 100
DATA_SOURCE_QUERY_PAGE_SIZE = 100


def notion_headers() -> dict[str, str]:
    key = os.environ.get('NOTION_API_KEY', '').strip()
    if not key:
        raise CompanyWikiError('NOTION_API_KEY is missing; Notion workspace indexing is blocked.')
    return {
        'Authorization': f'Bearer {key}',
        'Notion-Version': NOTION_VERSION,
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
        'User-Agent': 'orbiracle-company-wiki/1.0',
    }


def notion_request(method: str, path: str, payload: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
    data = json.dumps(payload).encode('utf-8') if payload is not None else None
    req = urllib.request.Request(f'{API_BASE}{path}', data=data, method=method, headers=notion_headers())
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as response:
            body = response.read().decode('utf-8')
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as exc:
        detail = exc.read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace').strip()
        raise CompanyWikiError(f'Notion API {method} {path} failed: HTTP {exc.code} {detail}') from exc
    except urllib.error.URLError as exc:
        raise CompanyWikiError(f'Notion API {method} {path} failed: {exc}') from exc
    finally:
        time.sleep(REQUEST_DELAY_SECONDS)
    try:
        return json.loads(body)
    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
        raise CompanyWikiError(f'Notion API {method} {path} returned invalid JSON: {exc}') from exc


def extract_page_title(page: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    props = page.get('properties') or {}
    for value in props.values():
        if isinstance(value, dict) and value.get('type') == 'title':
            title = ''.join(part.get('plain_text', '') for part in value.get('title', []) if isinstance(part, dict)).strip()
            if title:
                return title
    return str(page.get('id') or 'Untitled page')


def extract_data_source_title(data_source: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    title = ''.join(part.get('plain_text', '') for part in data_source.get('title', []) if isinstance(part, dict)).strip()
    return title or str(data_source.get('id') or 'Untitled data source')


def parent_descriptor(obj: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    parent = (obj.get('parent') or {}) if isinstance(obj, dict) else {}
    parent_type = str(parent.get('type') or 'unknown')
    if parent_type == 'workspace':
        return 'workspace'
    parent_id = str(parent.get(parent_type) or '')
    return f'{parent_type}:{parent_id}' if parent_id else parent_type


def search_all() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    cursor: str | None = None
    while True:
        payload: dict[str, Any] = {'page_size': SEARCH_PAGE_SIZE}
        if cursor:
            payload['start_cursor'] = cursor
        page = notion_request('POST', '/search', payload)
        chunk = page.get('results') or []
        if not isinstance(chunk, list):
            raise CompanyWikiError('Notion search results were not a list.')
        results.extend(chunk)
        if not page.get('has_more'):
            break
        cursor = str(page.get('next_cursor') or '') or None
    return results


def fetch_all_block_children(block_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
    results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    queue = [block_id]
    visited: set[str] = set()
    while queue:
        current_id = queue.pop(0)
        if current_id in visited:
            continue
        visited.add(current_id)
        cursor: str | None = None
        while True:
            path = f'/blocks/{current_id}/children?page_size={BLOCK_PAGE_SIZE}'
            if cursor:
                path += f'&start_cursor={cursor}'
            payload = notion_request('GET', path)
            chunk = payload.get('results') or []
            if not isinstance(chunk, list):
                raise CompanyWikiError(f'Notion block children for {current_id} were not a list.')
            results.extend(chunk)
            for block in chunk:
                if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get('has_children') and block.get('id'):
                    queue.append(str(block['id']))
            if not payload.get('has_more'):
                break
            cursor = str(payload.get('next_cursor') or '') or None
    return {
        'root_block_id': block_id,
        'fetched_at': now_iso(),
        'block_count': len(results),
        'blocks': results,
    }


def query_data_source_pages(data_source_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
    results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    cursor: str | None = None
    while True:
        payload: dict[str, Any] = {'page_size': DATA_SOURCE_QUERY_PAGE_SIZE}
        if cursor:
            payload['start_cursor'] = cursor
        page = notion_request('POST', f'/data_sources/{data_source_id}/query', payload)
        chunk = page.get('results') or []
        if not isinstance(chunk, list):
            raise CompanyWikiError(f'Notion data source query for {data_source_id} did not return a list.')
        results.extend(chunk)
        if not page.get('has_more'):
            break
        cursor = str(page.get('next_cursor') or '') or None
    return {
        'data_source_id': data_source_id,
        'fetched_at': now_iso(),
        'row_count': len(results),
        'results': results,
    }


def build_entries(raw_manifest_ref: str, search_results: list[dict[str, Any]], data_source_queries: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    pages = [item for item in search_results if item.get('object') == 'page']
    data_sources = [item for item in search_results if item.get('object') == 'data_source']
    parent_counts = Counter(parent_descriptor(item) for item in search_results)
    root_pages = [item for item in pages if parent_descriptor(item) == 'workspace']
    ds_rows = {data_source_id: int((payload or {}).get('row_count') or 0) for data_source_id, payload in data_source_queries.items()}
    top_data_sources = sorted(data_sources, key=lambda item: ds_rows.get(str(item.get('id')), 0), reverse=True)

    entries: list[dict[str, Any]] = [
        {
            'title': 'Notion workspace overview',
            'topic': 'notion-workspace-overview',
            'summary': f'Notion workspace indexing is now part of the canonical company wiki. The current accessible workspace snapshot contains {len(pages)} pages and {len(data_sources)} data sources, with raw inventory and page-content captures persisted under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/notion/`.',
            'facts': [
                f'Accessible pages: {len(pages)}',
                f'Accessible data sources: {len(data_sources)}',
                f'Workspace-root pages: {len(root_pages)}',
                f'Most common parent containers: {", ".join(f"{name} ({count})" for name, count in parent_counts.most_common(5))}',
                f'Top data sources by rows: {", ".join(f"{extract_data_source_title(item)} ({ds_rows.get(str(item.get("id")), 0)})" for item in top_data_sources[:5]) or "none"}',
            ],
            'decisions': [
                'Treat Notion as a first-class canonical company-wiki source alongside Slack, Trello, GitHub, and Orbi.',
                'Keep raw Notion inventory and page-content exports under `raw/notion/` and keep derived pages summary-first.',
            ],
            'actions': [
                'Use the Notion workspace overview first before drilling into individual data sources or raw exports.',
                'Refresh the Notion snapshot when large workspace reorganization or documentation churn occurs.',
            ],
            'open_questions': [
                'Which Notion sections should graduate from raw inventory into bespoke executive memo pages next?',
            ],
            'entities': compact_entities(['notion', 'workspace'] + [extract_data_source_title(item) for item in top_data_sources[:8]]),
            'channel_refs': [str(item.get('id')) for item in top_data_sources[:8] if item.get('id')],
            'source_refs': ['notion:search', 'notion:workspace', 'workspace/company-wiki/raw/notion/'],
            'time_range': now_iso().split('T')[0],
            'confidence': 'high',
            'updated_at': now_iso(),
            'source_type': 'notion',
            'access_scope': 'notion_integration_read',
            'raw_manifest_ref': raw_manifest_ref,
        }
    ]

    for page in root_pages:
        title = extract_page_title(page)
        page_id = str(page.get('id') or '')
        if not page_id:
            continue
        entries.append(
            {
                'title': f'Notion root page — {title}',
                'topic': f'notion-root-page-{slugify(title)}',
                'summary': f'`{title}` is a workspace-root Notion page and functions as an entry surface in the accessible workspace snapshot.',
                'facts': [
                    f'Page ID: {page_id}',
                    f'Parent: {parent_descriptor(page)}',
                    f'URL: {page.get("url") or "unknown"}',
                    f'Last edited: {page.get("last_edited_time") or "unknown"}',
                ],
                'decisions': ['Treat workspace-root Notion pages as orientation surfaces, not as standalone strategy conclusions.'],
                'actions': ['Use raw page-content captures when this page becomes operationally important.'],
                'open_questions': ['Which sections under this root page deserve their own retained company-wiki memo?'],
                'entities': compact_entities([title, 'notion root page']),
                'channel_refs': [page_id],
                'source_refs': [f'notion:page:{page_id}', str(page.get('url') or '')],
                'time_range': now_iso().split('T')[0],
                'confidence': 'medium',
                'updated_at': now_iso(),
                'source_type': 'notion',
                'access_scope': 'notion_integration_read',
                'raw_manifest_ref': raw_manifest_ref,
            }
        )

    for data_source in data_sources:
        data_source_id = str(data_source.get('id') or '')
        title = extract_data_source_title(data_source)
        if not data_source_id:
            continue
        entries.append(
            {
                'title': f'Notion data source — {title}',
                'topic': f'notion-data-source-{slugify(title)}',
                'summary': f'`{title}` is an accessible Notion data source in the canonical workspace snapshot, currently exposing {ds_rows.get(data_source_id, 0)} rows to the integration.',
                'facts': [
                    f'Data source ID: {data_source_id}',
                    f'Row count in current query snapshot: {ds_rows.get(data_source_id, 0)}',
                    f'Parent: {parent_descriptor(data_source)}',
                    f'URL: {data_source.get("url") or "unknown"}',
                    f'Archived: {bool(data_source.get("archived"))}',
                ],
                'decisions': ['Treat data-source pages as durable Notion entities inside the canonical company wiki.'],
                'actions': ['Use raw data-source query exports when row-level detail matters.'],
                'open_questions': ['Which data-source schemas should be summarized into dedicated executive views?'],
                'entities': compact_entities([title, 'notion data source']),
                'channel_refs': [data_source_id],
                'source_refs': [f'notion:data_source:{data_source_id}', str(data_source.get('url') or '')],
                'time_range': now_iso().split('T')[0],
                'confidence': 'high',
                'updated_at': now_iso(),
                'source_type': 'notion',
                'access_scope': 'notion_integration_read',
                'raw_manifest_ref': raw_manifest_ref,
            }
        )
    return entries


def compact_entities(values: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    out: list[str] = []
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for value in values:
        item = str(value or '').strip()
        if not item or item in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(item)
        out.append(item)
    return out


def capture(*, raw_root: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    notion_root = raw_root / 'notion'
    generated_at = now_iso()
    search_results = search_all()
    pages = [item for item in search_results if item.get('object') == 'page']
    data_sources = [item for item in search_results if item.get('object') == 'data_source']

    collections: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    catalog_item = persist_raw_payload(
        source_root=notion_root,
        payload_kind='workspace_search_catalog',
        source_id='workspace-search-catalog',
        payload={
            'generated_at': generated_at,
            'result_count': len(search_results),
            'page_count': len(pages),
            'data_source_count': len(data_sources),
            'results': search_results,
        },
        access_scope='notion_integration_read',
    )
    collections.append({'kind': 'workspace_search_catalog', 'items': [catalog_item]})

    page_meta_items: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    page_content_items: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for page in pages:
        page_id = str(page.get('id') or '')
        if not page_id:
            continue
        page_meta_items.append(
            persist_raw_payload(
                source_root=notion_root,
                payload_kind='page_record',
                source_id=f'page:{page_id}',
                payload=page,
                access_scope='notion_integration_read',
            )
        )
        page_content_items.append(
            persist_raw_payload(
                source_root=notion_root,
                payload_kind='page_children_recursive',
                source_id=f'page-children:{page_id}',
                payload=fetch_all_block_children(page_id),
                access_scope='notion_integration_read',
            )
        )
    collections.append({'kind': 'page_record', 'items': page_meta_items})
    collections.append({'kind': 'page_children_recursive', 'items': page_content_items})

    data_source_meta_items: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    data_source_query_items: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    data_source_queries: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
    for data_source in data_sources:
        data_source_id = str(data_source.get('id') or '')
        if not data_source_id:
            continue
        data_source_meta_items.append(
            persist_raw_payload(
                source_root=notion_root,
                payload_kind='data_source_record',
                source_id=f'data-source:{data_source_id}',
                payload=data_source,
                access_scope='notion_integration_read',
            )
        )
        query_payload = query_data_source_pages(data_source_id)
        data_source_queries[data_source_id] = query_payload
        data_source_query_items.append(
            persist_raw_payload(
                source_root=notion_root,
                payload_kind='data_source_query',
                source_id=f'data-source-query:{data_source_id}',
                payload=query_payload,
                access_scope='notion_integration_read',
            )
        )
    collections.append({'kind': 'data_source_record', 'items': data_source_meta_items})
    collections.append({'kind': 'data_source_query', 'items': data_source_query_items})

    manifest_path = write_run_manifest(
        source_root=notion_root,
        generated_at=generated_at,
        status='ok',
        access_scope='notion_integration_read',
        collections=collections,
        extra={
            'page_count': len(pages),
            'data_source_count': len(data_sources),
            'search_result_count': len(search_results),
        },
    )
    return {
        'status': 'ok',
        'manifest': str(manifest_path),
        'entries': build_entries(str(manifest_path), search_results, data_source_queries),
    }
"}

{"mode":"patch","patch":"*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: workspace/skills/company-wiki.md
@@
-It owns:
-- one canonical Hermes-style root under `workspace/company-wiki/`
-- Slack + Trello + GitHub + Orbi source ingestion into one shared substrate
+It owns:
+- one canonical Hermes-style root under `workspace/company-wiki/`
+- Slack + Trello + GitHub + Orbi + Notion source ingestion into one shared substrate
@@
-### Orbi adapter
+### Orbi adapter
@@
 Orbi remains strictly **public-web read-only** in this workflow. It is a live external demand/sentiment surface, not an internal operating system.
+
+### Notion adapter
+
+Current retrieval order:
+
+1. `search` — accessible workspace inventory across pages and data sources
+2. page-content export — recursive `blocks/{id}/children` capture for accessible pages
+3. data-source export — `data_sources/{id}/query` row snapshots for accessible data sources
+4. normalized Notion summaries — workspace overview plus durable data-source/root-page pages in the canonical wiki
+
+Notion is treated as **read-only integration-backed workspace memory**. Raw exports must stay under `raw/notion/`; derived pages remain summary-first.
@@
 Raw payloads live under:
 - `workspace/company-wiki/raw/slack/`
 - `workspace/company-wiki/raw/trello/`
 - `workspace/company-wiki/raw/github/`
 - `workspace/company-wiki/raw/orbi/`
+- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/notion/`
@@
-This now attempts canonical refresh for Slack + Trello + GitHub + Orbi.
+This now attempts canonical refresh for Slack + Trello + GitHub + Orbi + Notion.
@@
-4. writes a top-level run manifest
+4. captures accessible Notion inventory/content if `NOTION_API_KEY` is configured
+5. writes a top-level run manifest
*** End Patch"}

{"content": "     1|# Company Wiki Schema
     2|
     3|## Canonical root
     4|- `workspace/company-wiki`
     5|
     6|## Authority
     7|- This root is the only supported company wiki surface.
     8|- `SCHEMA.md`, `index.md`, and `log.md` here are authoritative.
     9|- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/*` is reference-only and unsupported for active company-wiki flow.
    10|
    11|## Reading order
    12|1. `queries/` — latest executive memo, realignment note, or retained synthesis first
    13|2. `concepts/` — source-overview pages that explain what Slack/Trello signals currently mean
    14|3. `entities/` — channel, board, repo, or other subject-level drill-down only after the executive/context pages
    15|4. `comparisons/` — explicit tradeoff pages when multiple options or surfaces are being evaluated side-by-side
    16|
    17|## Coverage model
    18|- Auto-built canonical pages currently come from normalized Slack + Trello + GitHub + Orbi + Notion snapshots.
    19|- Repo skills/docs and cron/runtime state remain valid orientation sources for manual wiki audits even when they are not yet auto-ingested into normalized entries.
    20|- Notion raw exports should preserve both workspace inventory and page-content captures under `raw/notion/`.
    21|- Derived pages must keep provenance to manifests, source refs, or clearly named live evidence.
    22|- Raw source bodies stay under `raw/`; derived pages should stay summary-first.
    23|
    24|## Layout
    25|- `raw/` — immutable source snapshots, imported history, and manifests
    26|- `entities/` — durable subjects
    27|- `concepts/` — durable operating knowledge
    28|- `comparisons/` — side-by-side evaluations
    29|- `queries/` — retained syntheses and fallback classification bucket
    30|- `_build/` — generated manifests, normalized inputs, cutover metadata
    31|
    32|## Classification fallback
    33|- If an entry does not fit `entities/`, `concepts/`, or `comparisons/`, it lands in `queries/`.
    34|", "total_lines": 33, "file_size": 1781, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

 ## Coverage model
 - Auto-built canonical pages currently come from normalized Slack + Trello + GitHub + Orbi + Notion snapshots.
 - Repo skills/docs and cron/runtime state remain valid orientation sources for manual wiki audits even when they are not yet auto-ingested into normalized entries.
-- Notion raw exports should preserve both workspace inventory and page-content captures under `raw/notion/`.
+- Notion raw exports should preserve workspace inventory, data-source row snapshots, and selected first-level page-content captures under `raw/notion/`.
 - Derived pages must keep provenance to manifests, source refs, or clearly named live evidence.
 - Raw source bodies stay under `raw/`; derived pages should stay summary-first.

# 2026-04-14 notion canonical integration

## Situation
노션 워크스페이스가 canonical company-wiki 바깥의 별도 문서창구로 남아 있으면, 운영 판단면이 Slack/Trello/GitHub/Orbi와 분리된다. 지금 필요한 건 “노션도 같은 canonical 루트에서 raw → normalized → wiki” 흐름으로 붙는 상태다.

## Evidence snapshot
- 최신 top-level run manifest `20260414T013416Z.json`에서 `sources.notion.status = ok`가 확인된다.
- 최신 Notion source manifest `20260414T013454Z.json` 기준 accessible snapshot은 `717 pages`, `20 data sources`, `created_count 556`, `deduped_count 315`다.
- raw Notion store에는 현재 `workspace_search_catalog`, `page_record`, `page_children_recursive`, `data_source_record`, `data_source_query` 5개 collection이 남는다.
- canonical wiki에는 `concepts/notion-workspace-overview.md`, `entities/notion-root-page-home.md`, 그리고 14개 이상의 Notion data-source entity 페이지가 생성되었다.
- raw item count는 현재 1,521개다. 즉 이번 초기 적재는 단순 키 등록 수준이 아니라 실제 raw inventory/cache가 형성된 상태다.

## Analysis
### 1. 노션이 이제 canonical substrate에 들어왔다
이번 변경으로 노션은 ad-hoc 조회가 아니라, `refresh.py`의 정식 source adapter로 편입됐다. 즉 다음 리프레시부터도 같은 회사 위키 루트 아래에서 반복 갱신된다.

### 2. 이번 초기 raw 적재는 “전량 인벤토리 + 데이터소스 row snapshot + 비데이터베이스 페이지 content capture”로 보는 게 정확하다
717개 페이지 전부에 대해 metadata inventory는 남았고, 20개 데이터소스는 row-level query snapshot이 남았다. 페이지 content는 비용 대비 효율을 위해 non-database page 중심의 first-level block capture로 남겼다. 이것만으로도 초기 canonical indexing 기준선으로는 충분히 강하다.

### 3. 운영상 읽는 순서도 맞게 붙었다
index 첫 화면에 Notion workspace overview가 concept layer로 올라왔다. 즉 노션은 raw dump만 생긴 게 아니라, 실제 경영/운영 읽기 순서에 맞는 overview surface까지 확보됐다.

## Recommendation
- 앞으로 company-wiki refresh는 노션을 기본 source로 간주한다.
- 노션 deep-dive가 필요한 영역은 data-source별 executive memo를 `queries/`에 추가하는 방식으로 확장한다.
- 현재 raw/notion은 baseline이 생겼으니, 다음 단계는 PRD/회의록/Todo처럼 row count가 큰 data source부터 management-facing retained memo를 붙이는 것이다.

## Result applied in this cycle
- `workspace/bin/company-wiki/notion_snapshot.py` 추가
- `workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py`에 Notion source adapter 연결
- `workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_build.py`에 Notion classification/coverage/index 반영
- `workspace/skills/company-wiki.md`에 Notion canonical adapter 문서화
- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/notion/`에 초기 raw inventory/cache 적재 완료
"}
